title: Indian Central Committee: Supplementary Note
publisher: Government of India Central Publication Branch
description: An authoritative political and legal supplementary note analyzing minority group representation, separate electorates for Muslim communities, and constitutional advance models across British India.

title: Calcutta and Environs: An Illustrated Historical Guide to Places of Interest and to Those Suitable for Excursions in and Around Calcutta
description: A comprehensive historical guide detailing the prominent state buildings, public institutions, monuments, and suburbs of Calcutta. It outlines the city's structural evolution, physical geography, and town-planning systems for tourists and newcomers.

title: A Critical Survey of the Development of the Urdu Novel and Short Story
description: A rigorous scholarly thesis investigating the historical evolution of Urdu prose fiction from classical verse fables and prose romances to the emergence of modern realistic novels and independent short stories.

title: A Grammar of Arabic Language
description: A refined pedagogical Arabic grammar textbook designed for Indian university students that models classical orthography, etymology, and syntax rules onto a concise and systematic European formatting scheme.

title: আমার জীবন: বিভাগপূর্ব বাংলাদেশের রাজনীতি (My Life: Politics of Pre-Partition Bangladesh)
publisher: চিরায়ত প্রকাশন প্রাইভেট লিমিটেড (Chirayata Prakashan Private Limited)
description: A detailed political memoir analyzing the democratic currents, internal conflicts, and socio-political transformations of Muslim politics and anti-colonial movements in Bengal between 1936 and 1947.

title: Agrarian Reform and Social Transformation: Preconditions for Development
description: A structural comparative analysis of land distribution systems, peasant mobilizations, and agricultural policy shifts across Latin America, Asia, and Africa evaluating the macro-economic results of radical versus non-egalitarian reforms.

title: An Introduction to the History of Sufism: The Sir Abdullah Suhrawardy Lectures for 1942 (Classic Edition)
description: An academic survey tracking Western scholarly research into Islamic mysticism across two historical phases, embellished with an introduction exploring the development and general structural principles of Sufi orders in India.

title: An Introduction to the History of Sufism: The Sir Abdullah Suhrawardy Lectures for 1942 (Original Edition)
description: The original wartime publication of the Sir Abdullah Suhrawardy Lectures exploring Islamic mysticism research trends and defining future scholarly guidelines for compiling original Sufi source texts and manuscripts.

title: Arabic Made Easy
description: A highly practical and structured educational textbook outlining the core rules of Arabic grammar, orthography, and composition through simplified weekly lessons, illustrations, and conversational exercises.

title: এসো তারার রাজ্যে (Come to the Realm of Stars)
publisher: বাংলাদেশ জনশিক্ষা পরিদপ্তর ও ইউনিসেফ (Directorate of Public Instruction & UNICEF)
description: An engaging astronomical science book written for children and young adults that introduces the wonders of constellations and stars through a masterful blending of folk storytelling and scientific facts.

title: কুরপালা কাজী বংশের ইতিবৃত্ত (History of the Kazi Family of Kurpala)
description: A meticulous genealogical chronicle detailing the heritage, prominent historical figures, lineage connections, and societal contributions of the prominent Kazi family of Kurpala.

title: Autobiography of Dr. M. Khabir
description: A concise and factual personal memoir detailing the academic pursuits, medical practice, and cross-continental travels of a dedicated public health professional.

title: Autobiography of Alhaj Shamiuz Zaman
description: A deeply reflective personal history recording the childhood recollections, ancestral roots at Zamindar Bari in Goshaipur, and life experiences across decades of shifting regional borders.

title: In Illustrious Bonds: I and My Relatives (English Translation)
description: The definitive English translation of a comprehensive paternal and maternal family memoir tracking ancestral origins, personal migrations following the Partition, and extensive network connections in Dhaka. Translated by Areeb Uzzaman.

title: Gender and National Identity: Women and Politics in Muslim Societies
description: A sophisticated socio-political essay anthology examining the dynamic intersection of gender roles, anti-colonial national liberation movements, and structural identity formation across Muslim societies, featuring specialized research insights into the construction of Bengali national identity. Her specific work in the book is in chapter 4.

title: বিশ্রুতবন্ধনে: আমি ও আমার আত্মীয়স্বজন (In Illustrious Bonds: I and My Relatives - Original Edition)
description: The original Bengali historical volume detailing the author's extensive familial roots, tracking regional lineage connections, cultural milestones, and spiritual lineages across traditional estates.

title: আমার জীবন (My Life)
description: An intensive autobiographical narrative from a prominent Marxist historian and political theorist, tracing his intellectual development, academic teaching posts, and direct social observations across shifting political eras.

title: পূর্ববাঙলার ভাষা আন্দোলন ও তৎকালীন রাজনীতি ১ (Language Movement of East Bengal and Contemporary Politics, Vol. 1)
description: A monumental historical analysis investigating the socio-economic and political roots of the Language Movement in East Bengal, drawing from extensive primary source documentation and interviews to trace the cultural awakening.

title: বাংলাদেশে শিক্ষা ও সংস্কৃতি (Education and Culture in Bangladesh)
description: A collection of analytical essays assessing the structural crises within the national educational system and exploring its deep, codependent relationship with cultural developments.

title: সাম্প্রদায়িকতা (Essays on Communalism)
description: A pioneering collection of structural sociopolitical essays investigating the deep historical roots, institutional triggers, and social consequences of communal divisions and factionalism.

title: বাঙলাদেশে ধর্মের রাজনৈতিক ব্যবহার (Political Use of Religion in Bangladesh)
description: A collection of structural sociopolitical essays evaluating the historical manipulation of religious narratives by ruling classes during both the Pakistani era and post-independence Bangladesh to obstruct secular democratic movements.

title: সংস্কৃতির সংকট (Crisis of Culture)
description: A seminal mid-century intellectual text evaluating the identity crisis and structural fractures within East Bengali society, exposing the exploitation of communal divides to suppress native language and cultural self-determination.

title: ইশ্বরচন্দ্র বিদ্যাসাগর ও উনিশ শতকের বাঙালী সমাজ (Ishwarchandra Vidyasagar and 19th Century Bengali Society)
publisher: চিরায়ত প্রকাশন প্রাইভেট লিমিটেড (Chirayata Prakashan Private Limited)
description: A meticulous historical and materialist survey analyzing the educational, secularizing, and social reform movements led by Ishwarchandra Vidyasagar within the complex class realities of 19th-century colonial Bengal.

title: গণআদালত: অসমাপ্ত মুক্তি সংগ্রামের জের (People's Court: Consequences of an Unfinished Liberation Struggle)
description: An incisive series of analytical columns exploring the historical legacy of the Liberation War, the rise of political extremism, and the systemic mobilization of secular civic networks to protect constitutional integrity.

title: নারী প্রশ্ন প্রসঙ্গে (On the Question of Women)
description: An edited compilation of foundational ideological essays investigating the historical coordinates of women's oppression, institutional violence, and the role of grassroots secular networks in building genuine democratic liberation frameworks.

title: রাজনীতি শিক্ষা সংস্কৃতি (Politics, Education, Culture)
description: A robust collection of contemporary cultural criticisms evaluating the systemic administrative crises across national educational models and their corresponding impacts on national political expression.

title: Politics and Society in East Pakistan and Bangladesh
description: An essential English-language sociological compendium analyzing the underlying class dynamics, political cross-currents, and institutional transformations that triggered the structural breakdown of Pakistan and led to the creation of Bangladesh.

title: The Emergence of Bangladesh, Part Two: 1958-1971
description: A comprehensive historical tour de force mapping the critical economic exploitation channels, military dictatorships, language movement legacies, and revolutionary peasant mobilizations that ultimately birthed the independent state.

title: Language Movement in East Bengal
description: An authoritative English-language historical monograph documenting the baseline triggers, administrative directives, and profound civic impacts of the Language Movement as the primary root catalyst for national self-determination.

title: Poverty Trade of Dr. Yunus
description: A collection of ideological critiques and structural analyses arguing that international micro-credit models and NGO structures function as market-driven schemes that neutralize genuine grassroots political mobilization among the rural poor.

title: The Indian National Movement: Raja Ram Mohan Roy Memorial Lectures
description: An exhaustive analytical history tracking the internal class contradictions, bourgeois political tactics, and institutional maneuvers of the Indian National Congress and Muslim League that culminated in the 1947 Partition.

title: Regret
description: A collection of Urdu fiction novellas and short stories exploring themes of memory, personal accountability, and displacement in the aftermath of the Partition.

title: The Art of the Mussulmans in Spain
description: An essential art history compendium analyzing the architectural grammar, structural engineering, decorative motifs, and material culture of Islamic civilization across classical Spain. Introduction written by Naz Ikramullah Ashraf.

title: First Aid for India
description: A pioneering medical instruction handbook detailing essential emergency procedures, anatomy overviews, and trauma response techniques tailored for public health workers across India.

title: Privatisation in Bangladesh: An Agenda in Search of a Policy
description: An in-depth economic policy analysis tracking the multi-decade operational and fiscal results of privatizing State-Owned Enterprises (SOEs) in Bangladesh.

title: Fifty Years of Bangladesh: Economy, Politics, Society and Culture
description: A holistic, multi-disciplinary scholarly anthology examining the multi-faceted development trajectory, economic structural shifts, and cultural landscapes of Bangladesh over its first half-century.

title: عوارف المعارف (Awarif-ul-Ma'arif)
description: A classical text exploring the foundational spiritual codes, moral philosophies, behavioral ethics, and operational structures governing the traditional Sufi path.

title: تسهيل العروض في شرح دراية الادب (Tashil-ul-Arud fi Sharh Dirayat-ul-Adab)
publisher: غلام احمد تاجر কتب (Gholam Ahmed Booksellers)
description: A comprehensive classical commentary and pedagogical analysis mapping the structural rules, prosody metrics, and traditional layout guidelines of Arabic poetic composition.

title: A Grammar of the Arabic Language: Intended Chiefly for the Arabic Students of the Calcutta University
description: The landmark original 19th-century elementary Arabic grammar manual, meticulously tracking morphological paradigms and foundational syntactic frameworks for higher education settings.

title: First Steps in Muslim Jurisprudence: Consisting of Excerpts from Bakurat-al-Sa'd of Ibn Abu Zayd
description: A rigorous legal translation and comparative commentary text analyzing the core tenets of Islamic civil jurisprudence, succession rules, and family law matrices.

title: Memoirs of Huseyn Shaheed Suhrawardy with a brief account of his life and work
description: The personal political memoirs of the former Prime Minister of Pakistan, tracing his ancestral roots, his lifelong struggle for Hindu-Muslim unity, the Khilafat movement, and his internal critiques of post-partition governance systems.

title: Theorizing a Bengali Nation: Abul Hashim and the United Bengal Movement, 1937-1947
description: An intensive external political monograph exploring the philosophical and civic roots of the United Bengal Movement of 1947, focusing specifically on the structural vision of Abul Hashim.

title: In Retrospection
description: A comprehensive English-language autobiography charting the author's lifelong involvement in Bengal's pre-partition socio-political transformations, anti-colonial struggles, and his structural leadership within the provincial Muslim League.

title: The Creed of Islam or The Revolutionary Character of Kalima
description: An ideological and philosophical text examining the revolutionary social justice principles, egalitarian frameworks, and structural moral parameters embedded within the foundational Islamic creed.

title: Partners or Contractors? The Relationship Between Official Agencies and NGOs: Bangladesh Case Study
publisher: International NGO Training and Research Centre (INTRAC)
description: A detailed developmental policy draft assessing the institutional relationships, historical trajectories, and financial funding mechanisms between official state donor agencies and non-governmental organizations in Bangladesh.

title: Untranquil Recollections: Nation Building in Post-Liberation Bangladesh
description: An engaging oral and personal history memoir capturing the turbulent economic planning hurdles, institutional factionalism, and foundational state-building challenges of Bangladesh over its first critical years post-liberation.

title: Trade, Planning and Rural Development: Essays in Honour of Nurul Islam
description: An edited collection of structural economic development essays investigating trade strategies, central planning systems, and agrarian inequalities within the Global South, compiled as a tribute to Nurul Islam.

title: Globalisation and Gender: Changing Patterns of Women's Employment in Bangladesh
publisher: Centre for Policy Dialogue & The University Press Limited
description: A rigorous socio-economic compilation analyzing the structural changes, working conditions, and labor force dynamics governing women's employment in Bangladesh under the influence of early globalization macro-policies.

title: Borders Without Boundaries: Reimagining Asia
description: An insightful regional policy essay reimagining the fluid nature of human geography and economic integration in Asia, advocating for a transformation of hard borders into porous zones of shared commercial opportunity.

title: Debt Default to the Development Finance Institutions: The Crisis of State Sponsored Entrepreneurship in Bangladesh
description: An intensive multi-year economic survey monitoring the repayment crisis, massive debt default, and institutional design flaws that afflicted state-sponsored private entrepreneurship platforms in Bangladesh during the 1980s.

title: The Crisis of External Dependence: The Political Economy of Foreign Aid to Bangladesh
description: An intensive political economy study analyzing the macro-economic patterns of foreign aid to Bangladesh, highlighting the leverage exercised by external donors and the growth of an intermediary comprador class.

title: Trade, Planning and Rural Development: Essays in Honour of Nurul Islam
description: An edited collection of economic development essays exploring trade policies, centralized planning systems, and structural rural reform models within the Global South, compiled as an honorific volume for Nurul Islam.

title: Challenging the Injustice of Poverty: Agendas for Inclusive Development in South Asia
description: A profound economic and structural analysis arguing that persistent poverty across South Asia is rooted in inequitable and unjust social orders, proposing a comprehensive set of institutional reforms to empower marginalized populations.

title: The South Asian Challenge
description: An extensive economic and political anthology exploring regional development trends, globalization impacts, and governance crises across South Asian democracies, featuring a specialized chapter on avoiding false solutions in governance reforms.

title: উতল রোমন্থন: পূর্ণতার সেই বছরগুলো (Untranquil Recollections: The Years of Fulfilment)
description: The authorized Bengali translation of the prominent political economist's memoirs, detailing his personal experiences, intellectual contributions, and active social observations during the foundational state-building era of Bangladesh.

title: Bangladesh: Problems of Governance
description: A critical policy analysis reviewing the erosion of democratic rights, institutional dependencies on foreign aid, and the systemic challenges of building an entrepreneurial culture within both the public and private sectors of Bangladesh.

title: دیوان عبیدی (Diwan-i-Obaidi)
description: The compiled classical Persian and Urdu poetic divan of the eminent scholar and educationist, showcasing a masterful command of traditional metrical cadences, spiritual philosophies, and deep literary heritage.

title: Calcutta and Environs: An Illustrated Historical Guide to Places of Interest and to Those Suitable for Excursions in and Around Calcutta
description: A detailed historical and topographical guide mapping the civic buildings, public institutions, monuments, and sanitation frameworks of Calcutta to demonstrate the evolution of British administrative urban planning.

title: Address by Sir Hassan Suhrawardy, Vice-Chancellor at the Annual Convocation
description: An official academic address delivered by the Vice-Chancellor at the University of Calcutta's annual convocation, reflecting on public health advances, modern educational standards, and the social empowerment of women.

title: The Sayings of Muhammad
description: A celebrated collection of the traditional sayings and ethical teachings of the Prophet Muhammad, curated to present the foundational moral virtues and humanitarian principles of Islam to an international audience.

title: Bangladesh's Permanent Liberation Struggle: Constructing an Inclusive Democratic Society
description: The inaugural Professor Abdur Razzaq Distinguished Lecture examining the ongoing struggle to realize a truly just, inclusive democratic society in Bangladesh through the systemic democratization of economic and political opportunities.

title: Mussulman Culture
description: A refined English translation of Professor V. V. Bartold's classical Russian historical survey charting the multi-century evolution, architectural achievements, and intellectual frameworks of Islamic civilization.

title: Prefaces: Lectures on Art Subjects
description: A landmark compendium of aesthetic lectures and art historical reviews evaluating classical Islamic art styles, European aesthetic movements, and subcontinental structural artistic heritage.

title: Huseyn Shaheed Suhrawardy: A Biography (Oxford Edition)
description: An intimate, multi-decade biographical survey tracking the political legacy, legal career, and regional leadership of the former Prime Minister of Pakistan, tracing his efforts to mobilize Muslim Bengal.

title: Huseyn Shaheed Suhrawardy: A Biography (Original Edition)
description: The definitive original biographical volume mapping the complex continental coalitions, constitutional maneuvers, and federal alignment strategies implemented by Huseyn Shaheed Suhrawardy.

title: Behind the Veil: Ceremonies Customs and Colour
description: A cultural chronicle tracing the domestic life, wedding rituals, social etiquette codes, and traditional ceremonies of elite Muslim women in pre-partition India.

title: Letters to Neena
description: An engaging collection of cross-continental correspondence and letters explaining the baseline cultural struggles, constitutional foundations, and early political evolution of the newly formed state of Pakistan.

title: Women Religion and the Law
description: An incisive legal analysis assessing the constitutional rights, gender status, and structural application of religious personal laws within the judicial system of Bangladesh.

title: From Purdah to Parliament (Original Edition)
description: The milestone autobiography documenting the author's transformative personal and political journey from a traditional secluded childhood to serving as one of the first female parliamentarians in Pakistan.

title: From Purdah to Parliament (Revised Edition)
description: The updated and revised edition of the landmark personal history, preserving critical insights into the cultural shifts, political factions, and gender politics of the subcontinental transition.

title: Islam To-Day
description: A comprehensive collaborative anthology providing an overview of Islamic societies, cultures, and traditions across the globe, edited for a mid-20th-century international readership. He specifically authored chapter 15 titled “India”.

title: First Voices: Six Poets from Pakistan
description: A pioneering English-language poetry collection showcasing the voices of six foundational Pakistani poets, including verse from the prominent literary figure Shahid Suhrawardy.

title: Resurgent Bangladesh
description: An extensive collection of seventy-nine articles analyzing the evolving dimensions of Bangladesh, focusing on good governance, policy challenges, and the nation's resilient trajectory between 2015 and 2018.

title: Rhapsody
description: A literary collection exploring the human experience through poetry, focusing on themes of imagination, narrative, and shared cultural expression within the context of contemporary Bangladesh.

title: The Evolving International Horizon (Volume 1)
description: The first volume of a series examining shifts in international relations, regional diplomacy, and global strategic landscapes from the perspective of an experienced Bangladeshi diplomat.

title: The Making of the Prime Minister: H.S. Suhrawardy in an Anagram Polity (1947–1958)
publisher: Institute of Liberation Bangabandhu and Bangladesh Studies
description: A scholarly political monograph analyzing the trajectory of H.S. Suhrawardy during the critical foundational years of Pakistan, documenting his struggle for constitutional governance in an unstable political environment.

title: Memoirs of Huseyn Shaheed Suhrawardy: With a Brief Account of His Life and Work
description: The quintessential political memoirs detailing the legal career, legislative leadership, and political philosophy of the former Prime Minister of Pakistan, tracing his commitment to communal harmony and inclusive nationalism.

title: বাঙলাদেশে শিক্ষা ও সংস্কৃতি (Education and Culture in Bangladesh)
description: A rigorous analytical collection assessing the structural crises within the national educational system and their deep-seated, codependent relationship with the broader cultural development of Bangladesh.

title: The Emergence of Bangladesh: Class Struggles in East Pakistan (1947–1958)
description: A foundational sociopolitical text analyzing the early class struggles, peasant movements, and labor uprisings in East Pakistan that acted as the primary drivers for the eventual emergence of Bangladesh.

title: বাঙালী লেখিকার প্রথম হজ্ব বিবরণী (A Bengali Woman Writer's First Hajj Memoir)
description: A pioneering historical travelogue documenting the spiritual reflections, physical journey, and cultural insights of the first recorded Hajj memoir authored by a Bengali Muslim woman. Compiled and curated by Shamsunnahar Zaman.
